programmes.show_description: Widdershins: Engrossing mystery with Patricia Routledge as the lady sleuth, helped by Lost's Dominic Monaghan. Hetty is drawn to a country village by the death of Robert's uncle.
Heads, I Win. Tails, You Lose.: Harry and Fergus take on the case of a serial killer targeting random victims and leaving a coin in their mouths. Heads, I Win. Tails, You Lose.
Just Family: Another episode of the classic Essex comedy. Tracey is hurt when Darryl asks her to give up one of her prison visits so that his mum and dad can go and see him.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Baby Come Back: More jokes about Essex girls in the popular sitcom. When Sharon meets Dave, the telephone engineer and old flame, her thoughts turn to marriage and motherhood.
Just Family: Another episode of the classic Essex comedy. Tracey is hurt when Darryl asks her to give up one of her prison visits so that his mum and dad can go and see him.
Confidence: Chigwell comedy with the two sisters and their irrepressibly naughty neighbour. Dorien joins the Open University, and Sharon perseveres with her job at the bakery.
Second Fiddle: Quirky drama with the rogue antiques dealer. A violin virtuoso wants his prized Stradivarius turned into a fake, so Lovejoy asks a fiddler to help investigate.
The Colour of Mary: Lighthearted drama with the maverick dealer. Lovejoy is on the lookout for a billiard table once owned by Mary Queen of Scots - as is a disgraced snooker ace.
Fly the Flag: A conundrum for the old devil of an antiques dealer in the quirky drama series. What is an American flag from the War of Independence doing in the local church?
The Dance of Death: The amateur sleuth uncovers a dark secret when a contestant is murdered at a ballroom dancing competition.
Moving Target: The hugely popular crime drama series with a cast of the nation's favourites. The UCOS team reinvestigate a hit and run that left a cycle courier with amnesia.
Objects of Desire: The ever-popular crime drama with a cast of the nation's favourites. When the team reinvestigate the murder of an antiques dealer, Sandra meets an old flame.
Miracle in Crooked Lane: Offbeat mystery series starring the wonderful Alan Davies. A woman in a coma is sighted on her way to church in her home village. Creek is on the case.
An Anxious Aunt: Sylvia Fox goes to Umbria for the wedding of her niece, Alice. But the groom, Tommaso, disappears, leaving a woman's body in the lake at the bottom of his garden.
Day of the Dead: When the Brokenwood Small Business Association stage a Mexican 'Day of the Dead' night market they didn't bargain on a real dead body being part of it.
Miles and Chandler's latest case takes them deep into the world of modern art after the discovery of a flayed human face at Whitechapel Gallery.
Natural Causes: A gruesome case for Sharon Small as the feisty detective, with Liza Tarbuck as guest star. Havers is teamed up with DI Knight to investigate a drowning in Kent.
Moving Target: The hugely popular crime drama series with a cast of the nation's favourites. The UCOS team reinvestigate a hit and run that left a cycle courier with amnesia.
Motherland: When two small children are left in Miep and Jan's care, it strengthens Jan's ties to the resistance and brings up Miep's issues with her past abandonment.
Confidence: Chigwell comedy with the two sisters and their irrepressibly naughty neighbour. Dorien joins the Open University, and Sharon perseveres with her job at the bakery.
Sparring with Death: Final Chapter: A second death at the Wildemarsh Wellness Retreat leads Mattie to uncover a heart-wrenching tragedy, waiting to be avenged.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Thug on the Tyne: Part 2: After Rawton's cover seems to have been blown, Burnside finds himself in deep water.
Riot City: Conway mans the barricades in a riot situation, but Glaze could find himself facing the real thing.
Crime and Punishment: Hollis stumbles across some grave goings-on at a crematorium, while Brownlow confronts a ghost from his past.
Zero Tolerance: Inspector Monroe clamps down on anti-social behaviour among the youth of Sun Hill, but others suffer as a result of his single-mindedness.
Scoop: Conway becomes involved with a call girl, thanks to Boulton. But is the charming Linda all that she seems?
Nurse Crane and Trixie investigate when a mother abandons her children at the clinic. When the woman is sent to a neurologist, the results are devastating for the whole family.
Sister Julienne helps a woman from Pakistan who is unable to have children, but who has found to her shock that her husband is bringing a pregnant second wife into their home.

programmes.show_name: Catherine Cookson: A Dinner of Herbs
programmes.show_description: First in a gripping two-part drama adapted from Cookson's best-selling book in which a sailor's plans to start a new life with his son come to a tragic end.
Scoop: The body of a high-level government official's secretary is found at the bottom of a staircase - and Sister Boniface claims she was dead before the fall.
Look to the Lady - Part Two: The adventure continues for our quirky 1930s detective, memorably played by Peter Davison. Campion travels to Suffolk to protect a medieval chalice.
For Whom the Bell Tolls: Harry and Fergus travel to the convent of a silent order of nuns when a friend asks Harry to investigate a suspicious suicide, believing foul play is afoot.
River's Edge: Emilia Fox stars in a feature-length forensics mystery. The Lyell team investigate the shooting of a family who were innocently picnicking by a river.
Communal Living: More adventures with the ageing team of sleuths. As Brian struggles to control and conceal his desire to drink again, he finds the perfect refuge in a commune.
